info.title: Unified Approvals Connector
info.description:
The Unified Approvals Connector is a Fastify service that bridges Workfront (Redrock) and the Unified Approvals system. It manages document-version approvals, multi-stage workflows, participant management, template CRUD, and decision-making.

## Authentication

The connector supports three authentication methods:

| Method | Header/Cookie | Use case |
|--------|--------------|----------|
| **WorkfrontAuthCookie** | `wf-auth` cookie | Browser-based Workfront sessions |
| **WorkfrontAuthHeader** | `wf-auth` header | Server-to-server calls from Workfront services |
| **Bearer** | `Authorization: Bearer <token>` | IMS Bearer token from Adobe IO / Developer Console |

All endpoints accept any of the three auth methods.

For Bearer token auth, the following Workfront context headers are also required:
- `wf-subdomain` or `x-gw-subdomain` — customer Workfront subdomain
- `wf-customerid` — Workfront customer ID
- `wf-userid` — Workfront user ID
- `wf-lane` — Workfront deployment lane (e.g. `prod`, `devtest`)

## Asset Types

Endpoints currently only support `DOCV` (Workfront document version) as the `assetType` path parameter.


## Base URL

`https://workfront.adobe.io/workfront-approvals/`

## Endpoint Groups

| Tag | Base path | Auth |
|-----|-----------|------|
| Approvals | `/api/v1/approvals/` | Complex |
| Templates | `/api/v1/templates/` | Complex |
| Search | `/api/v1/search/` | Complex |
| Bots | `/api/v1/bots/` | Complex |

tags.name: Decisions
tags.description: Make and delete decisions on approval stages

WorkfrontAuthCookie.name: wf-auth
WorkfrontAuthCookie.description: Workfront auth JWT issued as a session cookie

WorkfrontAuthHeader.name: wf-auth
WorkfrontAuthHeader.description: Workfront auth JWT passed as a request header

Bearer.description: IMS Bearer token from Adobe IO / Developer Console. When used, the following context headers are also required: `wf-subdomain` (or `x-gw-subdomain`), `wf-customerid`, `wf-userid`, `wf-lane`.

StageId.name: stageId
StageId.description: The ID of the approval stage

TemplateId.name: templateId
TemplateId.description: The ID of the approval template

XRequestId.name: x-request-id
XRequestId.description: Unique identifier for request tracing. Auto-generated if not provided.

XCorrelationId.name: x-correlation-id
XCorrelationId.description: Unique identifier for correlation across multiple service calls.

Approval.description: An approval record for an asset

templateId.description: ID of the template used to create this approval, if any

structure.description: DAG structure defining stage ordering and trigger dependencies

isEsmAsset.description: Whether the asset is an ESM (Experience Storage Manager) asset

frameSyncStatus.description: Whether the Frame.io asset sync is complete

userInfo.description: Contextual information about the authenticated user's relationship to this approval

activeDecision.description: The current user's most recent decision

availableDecisions.description: Map of decision key to display label for the current user

parentIds.description: IDs of stages that must complete before this stage activates

customMessage.description: Optional message sent to participants when this stage is activated

requesterId.description: ID of the user who added this participant

autoLockEnabled.description: Automatically lock the stage when all participants have decided

decisionRules.description: Override rule for when the stage is considered decided. `onlyOneDecisionRequired` marks the stage complete when any one participant decides.

StageTrigger.description: Defines when and how a stage is automatically activated

when.description: `onCreate` activates the stage immediately when the approval is created. `onStagesComplete` activates after specified parent stages complete.

stages.description: Stage IDs that must complete to fire this trigger (used with `onStagesComplete`)

allowedDecisions.description: Decisions that count as 'complete' for this trigger

ApprovalStructure.description: Represents one node in the stage dependency graph

StageBody.description: Stage definition used when creating or updating an approval

customMessage.description: Optional message for participants when this stage activates

ParticipantBody.description: Participant definition used in request bodies

TemplateBody.description: Request body for creating or updating a template

errors.description: Validation error details (present on 400 FST_ERR_VALIDATION errors)

response.description: Upstream service error response, if applicable

UnauthorizedResponse.description: Unauthorized — no valid auth credentials provided

BadRequestResponse.description: Bad Request — validation failure or invalid state

get.description: Get approval details for an asset.

post.description: Creates an approval for an ESM document with the given stage data. If a `templateId` is provided the stage structure is loaded from that template.

templateId.description: Optional template ID to base the approval structure on

delete.description: Deletes the approval for the given asset. The asset must be in an editable state.

204.description: Approval deleted successfully — no content returned

get.description: Returns suggested approval payloads derived from prior document versions, shaped for use when creating a new approval on the current asset. Results are ordered newest version first.

sourceVersion.description: Document version number this suggestion was derived from

post.description: Creates an approval with the given stage data. Returns 201 on success.

put.description: Replaces all stages on an existing approval with the given stage data. The asset must be in an editable state.

patch.description: Partially updates any field on the specified stage. The asset must be in an editable state.

delete.description: Deletes the specified stage from the approval. The asset must be in an editable state.

put.description: Locks the specified stage. All decisions on the stage must have been made before locking. The asset must be in an editable state.
Unlocks the specified stage, making it editable again. The asset must be in an editable state.

post.description: Makes a decision on one or more stages. If the decision already exists it will be updated. Overall stage and approval status is recalculated after each decision. Throws an error if the approval is not found or the current user is not a participant.

decision.description: The decision value (e.g. `approved`, `needs work`, `approved with changes`, `reviewed`)

stageIds.description: Stage IDs to apply the decision to. Defaults to all active stages if omitted.

delete.description: Removes the current user's decision(s) from all active stages. The approval must be active.

post.description: Makes a decision on a specific stage. Equivalent to `POST /decisions` with a single stage ID.

delete.description: Removes the current user's decision from the specified stage.

get.description: Returns participant suggestions derived from the previous document version's approval.

prevVersionWithApprovers.description: The prior document version that had approvers

post.description: Adds one or more participants to an approval. If the approval does not yet exist, one will be created. If a participant already exists they will be merged and updated. The asset must be in an editable state.

delete.description: Removes a participant from the approval. If all participants are removed, the stage and approval are also deleted. The asset must be in an editable state.

post.description: Sends a reminder notification to the specified participant. The approval must exist and be in an editable state.
Sends reminder notifications to all participants across all stages who have not yet made a decision.
Sends a reminder notification to a specific participant within a specific stage.

post.summary: Remind undecided participants on a stage
post.description: Sends reminder notifications to all undecided participants within a specific stage.

get.description: Returns the event history for an approval.

post.description: If a body with asset IDs is provided, returns approvals for those assets. Otherwise returns all approvals on projects owned by the current user that are in the current status.

put.description: Locks the stage and overall approval for the given asset IDs. All decisions on the stage must have been made before locking.
Unlocks the stage and overall approval for the given asset IDs.

get.description: Checks whether a Frame.io asset referenced by the given Workfront document version ID has finished syncing and is ready for use.

200.description: Frame API response — array of matching assets

post.description: Searches approvals with rich filtering. Supports cursor-based pagination.

folderId.description: Workfront folder ID(s). Use `null` to search the root folder.

stageStatus.description: Stage status value(s) to filter by

participantStatus.description: Participant status value(s) to filter by

view.description: View preset that controls which fields are returned

cursor.description: Pagination cursor from a previous response

items.description: Approval object shape may vary by `view` parameter

cursor.description: Pass to the next request to fetch the next page

post.description: Searches approvals using the same filter set as `POST /api/v1/approvals/search` but routed through the dedicated search service.

get.description: Returns all approval templates visible to the current user.

post.description: Creates an approval template. A customer can create a maximum of 1000 templates. Requires template management permissions.

get.description: Returns a single approval template by ID.

put.description: Fully replaces an approval template. Requires template management permissions.

patch.description: Partially updates an approval template. Requires template management permissions.

delete.description: Deletes a single approval template. Requires template management permissions.
Deletes multiple approval templates in one call. IDs are comma-separated in the path. Requires template management permissions.

parameters.name: ids
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of template IDs to delete

get.description: Returns a paginated list of bot accounts available in the current customer's Workfront instance.
